arts.ttl: ‘Irreplaceable’: Pep Guardiola praises Rodri as City wait for injury diagnosis
arts.athr: Jamie Jackson at the Etihad Stadium
arts.sum: ‘He will be out for a long time. We wait for the doctors’ Rodri sustained suspected ACL injury against Arsenal Pep Guardiola will find out on Wednesday if he will be without Rodri for the rest of the season and has described his star midfielder as “irreplaceable”. The 28-year-old sustained a suspected anterior cruciate ligament injury 21 minutes into the 2-2 draw with Arsenal at the Etihad Stadium on Sunday. Guardiola, after his much-changed side beat Watford 2-1 in the Carabao Cup third round, was asked how long Rodri may be out. “Still we don’t have the definitive [diagnosis] but he will be out for a long time, a while,” Guardiola said. “But there are some opinions that maybe it will be less than we expected. Continue reading...

arts.sum: Man City 2-1 Watford: Nunes scored the winner after Jeremy Doku’s early strike

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola provides Rodri update with Man City left sweating over the severity of the Spanish star's knee injury suffered in 2-2 draw with Arsenal
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola has provided an update on the length of Rodri 's expected lay-off after the midfielder sustained a serious knee injury against Arsenal on Sunday.

arts.ttl: Rodri injury: Progress and potential return date for Man City linchpin
arts.sum: Guardiola did not then know the severity, but reports emerged the following day which suggested Rodri had torn his ACL. Arsenal proved themselves to be City's equals on enemy territory, and their title aspirations have only been emboldened amid speculation regarding the severity of Rodri's injury.

arts.ttl: Man City made to wait for full extent of Rodri injury results
arts.sum: No news for the Premier League champions who could be without a key player all year

arts.ttl: Man City's plan to cope without Rodri: Four players in line to replace his role, a change in formation... and could they recall Kalvin Phillips or return for Newcastle's Bruno Guimaraes?
arts.sum: Rodri is arguably City's most important player, dictating the tempo of the game week after week, but Guardiola must now find a different solution in the middle of the park.
